verses.text: David and his men return to Ziklag to find it burned and their wives and children carried off by raiding Amalekites. The refuge he chose in fear has become a heap of ashes. The compromises that promise safety often leave us, in the end, with the deepest loss.

David and his men return to Ziklag to find it burned and their wives and children carried off by raiding Amalekites. The refuge he chose in fear has become a heap of ashes. The compromises that promise safety often leave us, in the end, with the deepest loss.

David and his men return to Ziklag to find it burned and their wives and children carried off by raiding Amalekites. The refuge he chose in fear has become a heap of ashes. The compromises that promise safety often leave us, in the end, with the deepest loss.

The men weep till they have no strength, and in their grief speak of stoning David. At his lowest, blamed and bereaved, 'David encouraged himself in the LORD his God.' When every prop is gone and even friends turn, faith finds its footing in God alone.

The men weep till they have no strength, and in their grief speak of stoning David. At his lowest, blamed and bereaved, 'David encouraged himself in the LORD his God.' When every prop is gone and even friends turn, faith finds its footing in God alone.

The men weep till they have no strength, and in their grief speak of stoning David. At his lowest, blamed and bereaved, 'David encouraged himself in the LORD his God.' When every prop is gone and even friends turn, faith finds its footing in God alone.

David calls for the ephod and asks the LORD whether to pursue, and God answers, 'Pursue: for thou shalt surely overtake them, and without fail recover all.' Before he runs, he asks. The habit of enquiring of God, kept through the years, steadies him in catastrophe.

David calls for the ephod and asks the LORD whether to pursue, and God answers, 'Pursue: for thou shalt surely overtake them, and without fail recover all.' Before he runs, he asks. The habit of enquiring of God, kept through the years, steadies him in catastrophe.

David sets out with six hundred, but two hundred are too exhausted to cross the brook Besor and stay behind with the supplies. Even in urgent pursuit, room is made for the weak. Not all can keep the front line; God's work has a place for the faint as well as the strong.

David sets out with six hundred, but two hundred are too exhausted to cross the brook Besor and stay behind with the supplies. Even in urgent pursuit, room is made for the weak. Not all can keep the front line; God's work has a place for the faint as well as the strong.

They find a half-dead Egyptian slave, abandoned by the Amalekites, and David revives him with food and water. In gratitude the man leads them to the raiders. A small mercy to a forgotten stranger becomes the very key to the victory. Kindness is never wasted.

They find a half-dead Egyptian slave, abandoned by the Amalekites, and David revives him with food and water. In gratitude the man leads them to the raiders. A small mercy to a forgotten stranger becomes the very key to the victory. Kindness is never wasted.

They come upon the Amalekites sprawled across the fields, eating and drinking and dancing over their plunder, and David falls on them. The careless revelry of the wicked is their undoing. Those who feast on stolen joy are seldom ready for the reckoning that comes.

They come upon the Amalekites sprawled across the fields, eating and drinking and dancing over their plunder, and David falls on them. The careless revelry of the wicked is their undoing. Those who feast on stolen joy are seldom ready for the reckoning that comes.

David recovers everything — every wife, every child, all the spoil; 'there was nothing lacking.' God had promised he would 'recover all,' and He kept His word to the letter. What faith pursues at God's command, God gives back in full.

The two hundred who stayed with the supplies are met coldly by some who would deny them a share. But David rules that those who guard the baggage share equally with those who fight: 'as his part is that goeth down to the battle.' In God's army, faithful service in any post is rewarded alike.

David sends portions of the spoil to the elders of Judah, to the towns where he had roamed as a fugitive. He shares his blessing widely and remembers those who befriended him. Generosity that gives back to others marks a heart already being shaped for kingship.

David sends portions of the spoil to the elders of Judah, to the towns where he had roamed as a fugitive. He shares his blessing widely and remembers those who befriended him. Generosity that gives back to others marks a heart already being shaped for kingship.

The gifts reach town after town across the south, binding the hearts of Judah to David before he ever takes the throne. He wins the people not by force but by open-handed kindness. The way to lasting influence is paved with generosity, not with grasping.
